title: Isotrema shimadae
taxonomy: Isotrema shimadae is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Taiwan and is a type of evergreen vine. This plant is also known as Shimada's Isotrema or Taiwan Isotrema. The classification of Isotrema shimadae places it within the genus Isotrema, which is a part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. Isotrema shimadae is considered to be an endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.

intro: Isotrema shimadae, also known as Shimada's Isotrema, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the subtropical regions of East Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as skin conditions and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Isotrema shimadae include alkaloids, such as isotremin, and other compounds with potential medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Isotrema shimadae is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, which is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
